title: Hieracium subviolascentiforme
taxonomy: Hieracium subviolascentiforme is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the Hieracium genus, which consists of approximately 1000 species of plants commonly known as hawkweeds. The genus Hieracium is further divided into several subgenera, with H. subviolascentiforme belonging to the subgenus Pilosella. This species is native to certain regions of the world and is often found growing in rocky or mountainous areas. The classification of H. subviolascentiforme is typically based on its morphological characteristics and genetic analysis.

intro: Hieracium subviolascentiforme, also known as a species of hawkweed, is a flowering plant characterized by its yellow or orange dandelion-like flowers and hairy stems. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can aid in relieving pain and reducing inflammation in the body. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium subviolascentiforme include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
